docs.title: Riku - The Smallest PaaS
docs.text:
The smallest PaaS you've ever seen (Rust edition)

Riku is a complete Rust port of the Piku micro-PaaS, providing Heroku-like git push deployments to small servers.

docs.title: 1. Install Riku
docs.text:
# Install Rust (if not already installed)
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh

# Clone and build Riku
git clone https://github.com/dreygur/riku.git
cd riku
cargo build --release
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: 2. Initialize Your Server
docs.text:
# Create deploy user
sudo adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' deploy
sudo su - deploy

# Initialize Riku
riku init

# Add your SSH key
riku setup ssh ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

docs.title: 3. Deploy Your First App
docs.text:
# Create your app
mkdir myapp && cd myapp
git init

# Create a simple Node.js app
echo '{"name":"test","scripts":{"start":"node server.js"}}' > package.json
echo 'console.log("Hello from port", process.env.PORT)' > server.js
echo 'web: node server.js' > Procfile

# Deploy
git add . && git commit -m "Initial commit"
git remote add riku deploy@your-server:myapp
git push riku master

docs.title: 🎯 Features
docs.text: Feature Description Git Push Deploy Deploy with git push - just like Heroku Multi-Language Python, Node.js, Ruby, Go, Java, Clojure, Rust Process Supervisor Custom Rust supervisor (no uWSGI needed) Auto Scaling Scale workers with SCALING file Nginx Integration Automatic config generation SSL/HTTPS Built-in ACME support Plugin System Extend with server & client plugins Cron Jobs Schedule tasks in Procfile Zero Downtime Graceful restarts & rolling deploys

docs.title: 📊 Resource Usage
docs.text:
Component Memory Storage Riku Supervisor 10-30 MB ~8 MB Per App Process 10-200 MB 10-500 MB Nginx 5-15 MB ~5 MB

Minimum Requirements: - CPU: 1 core (500 MHz+) - RAM: 256 MB (512 MB recommended) - Storage: 50 MB + app dependencies

docs.title: 📚 Documentation
docs.text: Section Description Installation Install Riku on your server Quick Start Deploy your first app in 5 minutes Environment Variables Configure your deployments Scaling Scale your applications Plugins Extend Riku's functionality FAQ Common questions answered

docs.title: 🚀 Supported Runtimes
docs.text:
PythonNode.jsGoRubyRust 
# requirements.txt
flask>=2.0.0

# Procfile
web: gunicorn app:app

# package.json
{
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js"
  }
}

# Procfile
web: node server.js

# go.mod
module example.com/myapp

# Procfile
web: ./server

# Gemfile
source 'https://rubygems.org'
gem 'puma'

# Procfile
web: bundle exec puma

# Cargo.toml
[package]
name = "myapp"

# Procfile
web: ./target/release/myapp

docs.title: 🏗️ Architecture
docs.text:
┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐
│ Git Client  │───▶│ Riku Server  │───▶│   Apps      │
│             │    │              │    │             │
│ git push    │    │  Supervisor  │    │  Managed by │
│ deployments │    │  (Rust)      │    │  Supervisor │
└─────────────┘    │              │    │             │
                   │  Nginx       │    └─────────────┘
                   │  (Reverse    │
                   │   Proxy)     │
                   └──────────────┘

docs.title: 🤝 Contributing
docs.text:
We welcome contributions! Here's how you can help:

  1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch (git checkout -b feature/my-feature)
  3. Make your changes
  4. Add tests for new features
  5. Run cargo test and cargo clippy
  6. Submit a pull request

See CONTRIBUTING.md for detailed guidelines.

docs.title: 📜 License
docs.text: Riku is released under the MIT License.

docs.title: 🔗 Links
docs.text:
• GitHub Repository: github.com/dreygur/riku
  • Original Piku: github.com/piku/piku
  • Rust Documentation: doc.rust-lang.org
  • Report Issues: GitHub Issues

docs.title: 💡 Why Riku?
docs.text:
Riku (Rust) Original Piku (Python) Single binary Requires Python runtime No runtime dependencies Python 3 required Memory safe Garbage collected Compile-time errors Runtime errors ~30 MB memory footprint ~100+ MB footprint Fast startup Slower startup

Riku stands on the shoulders of giants. We thank the Piku team for creating the original micro-PaaS that inspired this Rust port.

Ready to deploy? Check out the Quick Start Guide!

docs.title: General Questions
docs.text:
Q: Why Riku?

A: Riku is a complete Rust port of the original Piku micro-PaaS. The name honors the original project while signifying our independent implementation in Rust.

Q: Why Rust?

A: Rust provides several advantages over the original Python implementation: - Single binary deployment - No Python runtime dependencies - Memory safety - No garbage collection, no runtime errors - Performance - Compiled code runs faster with lower memory footprint - Type safety - Catch errors at compile time instead of runtime - Low resource usage - Ideal for small VPS and edge deployments

Q: Is Riku compatible with Piku?

A: Yes! Riku maintains compatibility with Piku's: - Directory structure (~/.riku/ instead of ~/.piku/) - Environment variable format - Procfile syntax - Plugin system - Git-based deployment workflow

Q: Does Riku use uWSGI?

A: No! Riku implements its own process supervisor in Rust, replacing uWSGI Emperor. This gives us: - Better control over process lifecycle - Native health checking - Built-in log rotation - Lower memory footprint - No Python dependencies

Q: What runtimes does Riku support?

A: Riku supports: - Python (requirements.txt, Poetry, uv) - Node.js (npm, yarn, pnpm) - Ruby (Gemfile) - Go (modules, Godeps) - Java (Maven, Gradle) - Clojure (CLI, Leiningen) - Rust (Cargo) - Static sites - Containers (Docker/Podman)

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
Q: How do I deploy an app?

A: Just like Heroku or Piku: 

git remote add riku deploy@your-server:myapp
git push riku master

Q: How do I scale my app?

A: Use the SCALING file: 

echo "web=4" > SCALING
echo "worker=2" >> SCALING
git add SCALING && git commit -m "scale"
git push riku master

Or use environment variables: 

riku config:set myapp RIKU_WORKER_PROCESSES="web=4,worker=2"

Q: How do I set environment variables?

A: Use the config command: 

riku config:set myapp KEY=value ANOTHER_KEY=value2

Or create an ENV file in ~/.riku/envs/myapp/ENV.

Q: Can I use custom nginx configuration?

A: Yes! Place nginx.conf, nginx.custom.conf, or .nginx.conf in your app directory, or use: 

NGINX_INCLUDE_FILE=custom.conf

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
Q: My app won't start. How do I debug?

A: Check the logs: 

riku logs myapp
riku logs myapp web

Q: How do I restart my app?

A: 

riku restart myapp

Or set PIKU_AUTO_RESTART=false and restart manually.

Q: Where are logs stored?

A: In ~/.riku/logs/<app>/. Logs are automatically rotated when they exceed 10MB.

Q: How do I backup my apps?

A: Use the backup plugin or manually: 

tar -czf backup.tar.gz ~/.riku/apps/myapp

docs.title: Advanced
docs.text:
Q: Can I run multiple apps on the same domain?

A: Yes, use different subdomains or paths with NGINX_SERVER_NAME and NGINX_STATIC_PATHS.

Q: How do I enable HTTPS?

A: Set the domain and enable HTTPS redirect: 

riku config:set myapp NGINX_SERVER_NAME=example.com NGINX_HTTPS_ONLY=true

Then obtain SSL certificates (ACME support is built-in).

Q: Can I use Riku with Cloudflare?

A: Yes! Set: 

NGINX_CLOUDFLARE_ACL=true

And create a Cloudflare Page Rule to "Always Use HTTPS".

Q: How do I add custom cron jobs?

A: Add to your Procfile: 

cron: 0 2 * * * /path/to/script.sh

Q: Does Riku support zero-downtime deployments?

A: Yes! The supervisor gracefully restarts workers. Set PIKU_AUTO_RESTART=true (default) for automatic restarts on deploy.

docs.title: Migration from Piku
docs.text:
Q: How do I migrate from Piku to Riku?

A: 1. Install Riku alongside Piku 2. Riku uses ~/.riku/ instead of ~/.piku/ 3. Copy your apps: cp -r ~/.piku/apps/* ~/.riku/apps/ 4. Copy your env files: cp -r ~/.piku/envs/* ~/.riku/envs/ 5. Update git remotes to point to Riku 6. Test each app and migrate gradually

Q: Are Piku plugins compatible with Riku?

A: Yes! Shell-based plugins work with both. Place them in ~/.riku/plugins/.

Q: What about uWSGI configuration?

A: Riku doesn't use uWSGI. Replace uWSGI-specific env vars with Riku equivalents: - UWSGI_PROCESSES → Use SCALING file or RIKU_WORKER_PROCESSES - UWSGI_MAX_REQUESTS → Not needed (handled by supervisor) - UWSGI_* → See docs/ENV.md for deprecated list

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
Q: What's the memory footprint?

A: Typical usage: - Riku supervisor: 10-30 MB - Riku binary: ~8 MB - Per app process: 10-200 MB (depends on runtime) - Nginx: 5-15 MB

Q: How many apps can I run?

A: Depends on your server resources. On a 512MB VPS, you can comfortably run 2-3 small apps. On 2GB, 10+ apps is feasible.

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
Q: Where do I get help?

A: - Open an issue on GitHub - Check the documentation in docs/ - Review example apps in examples/

Q: How do I contribute?

A: 1. Fork the repository 2. Create a feature branch 3. Add tests for new features 4. Submit a pull request

See CONTRIBUTING.md for details.

docs.title: TL;DR
docs.text:
To install Riku on your server, ssh in as root and run:

curl https://riku.github.io/get | sh

Or manually:

# Install Rust (if not already installed)
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh

# Clone and build Riku
git clone https://github.com/piku/piku.git
cd piku
cargo build --release
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

# Create deploy user
sudo adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' deploy
sudo su - deploy

# Initialize Riku
riku setup init

# Add your SSH public key
riku setup ssh ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

docs.title: Installation Methods
docs.text:
There are several ways to install Riku:

  1. Automated script - Use the installation script (recommended)
  2. Manual installation - Follow the guide below
  3. Cloud-init - Automatic VPS setup (see cloud-init repository)
  4. Ansible - Use the Ansible playbook (coming soon)
  5. Docker - Run Riku in a container (experimental)

docs.title: Minimum Requirements
docs.text:
• CPU: 1 core (500 MHz+)
  • RAM: 256 MB (512 MB recommended)
  • Storage: 50 MB for Riku + app dependencies
  • OS: Linux (Debian/Ubuntu/RHEL/Arch)

docs.title: Required Software
docs.text:
• Rust (for building from source)
  • Git (for deployments)
  • Nginx (for reverse proxy)
  • SSH (for remote access)

docs.title: Optional Software
docs.text:
• Node.js (for Node.js apps)
  • Python 3 (for Python apps)
  • Ruby (for Ruby apps)
  • Go (for Go apps)

docs.title: Step 1: Install Rust
docs.text:
Riku is written in Rust. You need Rust to build it:

curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
source $HOME/.cargo/env

docs.title: Step 2: Build Riku
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/piku/piku.git
cd piku
cargo build --release

docs.title: Step 3: Install the Binary
docs.text:
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/
sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/riku

docs.title: Step 4: Create Deploy User
docs.text:
Riku requires a separate user account:

sudo adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' deploy
sudo su - deploy

docs.title: Step 5: Initialize Riku
docs.text:
riku setup init

This creates the directory structure: 

~/.riku/
├── apps/              # Application code
├── envs/              # Environment variables
├── repos/             # Git repositories
├── logs/              # Application logs
├── nginx/             # Nginx configurations
├── cache/             # Nginx cache
├── workers/           # Worker configurations
├── workers-available/ # Available worker configs
├── workers-enabled/   # Enabled worker configs
└── plugins/           # Plugins

docs.title: Step 6: Set Up SSH Access
docs.text:
On your local machine, generate an SSH key if you don't have one:

ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "riku@your-email.com"

Copy your public key to the server:

ssh-copy-id deploy@your-server

Or manually add it:

# On the server
riku setup ssh ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub

docs.title: Step 7: Verify Installation
docs.text:
riku --help

You should see the Riku help message.

docs.title: Ubuntu/Debian
docs.text:
# Update system
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

# Install dependencies
sudo apt install -y git nginx curl build-essential

# Install Rust
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh

# Build and install Riku
git clone https://github.com/piku/piku.git
cd piku
cargo build --release
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

# Create user
sudo adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' deploy
sudo su - deploy

# Initialize
riku setup init

docs.title: CentOS/RHEL
docs.text:
# Install EPEL repository
sudo yum install -y epel-release

# Install dependencies
sudo yum install -y git nginx curl gcc

# Install Rust
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh

# Build and install Riku
git clone https://github.com/piku/piku.git
cd piku
cargo build --release
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

# Create user
sudo adduser -m deploy
sudo su - deploy

# Initialize
riku setup init

docs.title: Arch Linux
docs.text:
# Install dependencies
sudo pacman -S git nginx curl rust

# Build and install Riku
git clone https://github.com/piku/piku.git
cd piku
cargo build --release
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

# Create user
sudo useradd -m deploy
sudo su - deploy

# Initialize
riku setup init

docs.title: Enable Nginx
docs.text:
# On your local machine (as root or with sudo)
sudo systemctl enable nginx
sudo systemctl start nginx

docs.title: Configure Firewall
docs.text:
Allow SSH and HTTP/HTTPS:

# Ubuntu/Debian (ufw)
sudo ufw allow OpenSSH
sudo ufw allow 'Nginx Full'
sudo ufw enable

# CentOS/RHEL (firewalld)
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=ssh
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=http
sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=https
sudo firewall-cmd --reload

docs.title: Set Up DNS
docs.text:
Point your domain to your server's IP address:

A record: your-domain.com → your.server.ip
A record: *.your-domain.com → your.server.ip

docs.title: Testing Your Installation
docs.text:
Create a test app:

# Create test directory
mkdir ~/test-app && cd ~/test-app
git init

# Create a simple app
echo 'web: python3 -m http.server $PORT' > Procfile
echo 'NGINX_SERVER_NAME=test.example.com' > ENV

# Commit and deploy
git add .
git commit -m "test"
git remote add riku deploy@your-server:test-app
git push riku master

docs.title: Upgrading Riku
docs.text:
To upgrade to the latest version:

# Pull latest changes
cd ~/piku
git pull

# Rebuild
cargo build --release

# Install new binary
cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

# Restart supervisor
systemctl --user restart riku

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
To remove Riku:

# Stop services
systemctl --user stop riku
systemctl --user disable riku

# Remove binary
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/riku

# Remove Riku data (WARNING: deletes all apps!)
rm -rf ~/.riku

# Remove user (optional)
sudo userdel -r deploy

docs.title: Riku command not found
docs.text:
Ensure /usr/local/bin is in your PATH:

export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/bin

docs.title: Permission denied errors
docs.text:
Make sure you're running as the deploy user:

su - deploy

docs.title: Build fails
docs.text:
Ensure you have the latest Rust:

rustup update

docs.title: Nginx configuration errors
docs.text:
Test nginx configuration:

sudo nginx -t

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Documentation: See docs/ directory
  • Issues: Open an issue on GitHub
  • Examples: Check examples/ for sample apps

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: After installation: 1. Read docs/ENV.md for environment variable configuration 2. Check docs/PLUGINS.md for extending Riku 3. Deploy your first app!

docs.title: Quick Start Guide
docs.text: Get Riku up and running in 5 minutes!

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A Linux server (Ubuntu, Debian, CentOS, or Arch)
  • SSH access to your server
  • Git installed on your local machine
  • A domain name (optional, for HTTPS)

docs.title: Step 1: Install Riku on Your Server
docs.text:
SSH into your server and run:

# Install Rust (if not already installed)
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
source $HOME/.cargo/env

# Clone and build Riku
git clone https://github.com/dreygur/riku.git
cd riku
cargo build --release

# Install the binary
sudo cp target/release/riku /usr/local/bin/

docs.title: Step 2: Create Deploy User
docs.text:
# Create a dedicated user for deployments
sudo adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' deploy
sudo su - deploy

docs.title: Step 3: Initialize Riku
docs.text:
# Initialize Riku directory structure
riku init

# This creates:
# ~/.riku/apps/     - Application code
# ~/.riku/repos/    - Git repositories
# ~/.riku/envs/     - Environment variables
# ~/.riku/logs/     - Application logs
# ~/.riku/nginx/    - Nginx configurations
# ~/.riku/workers/  - Worker configurations

docs.title: On Your Local Machine
docs.text:
Generate an SSH key if you don't have one:

ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your-email@example.com"

docs.title: Add Your Key to the Server
docs.text:
# Copy your public key to the server
ssh-copy-id deploy@your-server-ip

# Or manually add it
riku setup ssh ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub

docs.title: Step 5: Install Nginx
docs.text:
Riku requires nginx as a reverse proxy:

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y nginx

# CentOS/RHEL
sudo yum install -y nginx

# Start nginx
sudo systemctl enable nginx
sudo systemctl start nginx

docs.title: Create a Simple App
docs.text:
# On your local machine
mkdir myapp && cd myapp
git init

docs.title: Create a Node.js App
docs.text:
# package.json
cat > package.json << 'EOF'
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node server.js"
  }
}
EOF

# server.js
cat > server.js << 'EOF'
const http = require('http');
const port = process.env.PORT || 3000;
const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {
  res.writeHead(200, {'Content-Type': 'text/plain'});
  res.end(`Hello from Riku! Port: ${port}\n`);
});
server.listen(port, '0.0.0.0', () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${port}`);
});
EOF

# Procfile
echo 'web: node server.js' > Procfile

docs.title: Deploy
docs.text:
# Add your Riku server as a remote
git remote add riku deploy@your-server-ip:myapp

# Deploy
git add . && git commit -m "Initial commit"
git push riku master

docs.title: Step 7: Access Your App
docs.text:
Your app should now be running! Access it at:

http://your-server-ip

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Task Command View logs riku logs myapp Scale workers echo "web=4" > SCALING && git push riku master Set env vars riku config:set myapp KEY=value Restart app riku restart myapp Stop app riku stop myapp

docs.title: Set DNS
docs.text:
Point your domain to your server:

A record: example.com → your-server-ip
A record: *.example.com → your-server-ip

docs.title: Configure Riku
docs.text:
# Set domain name
riku config:set myapp NGINX_SERVER_NAME=example.com

# Enable HTTPS (after getting SSL cert)
riku config:set myapp NGINX_HTTPS_ONLY=true

docs.title: App Won't Start
docs.text:
# Check logs
riku logs myapp

# Check if app is running
riku ps myapp

# Restart
riku restart myapp

docs.title: Can't Access App
docs.text:
# Check nginx status
sudo systemctl status nginx

# Check firewall
sudo ufw status  # Ubuntu/Debian
sudo firewall-cmd --list-all  # CentOS/RHEL

# Check if port 80 is open
sudo netstat -tlnp | grep :80

docs.title: Deployment Fails
docs.text:
# Check git remote
git remote -v

# Verify SSH access
ssh deploy@your-server-ip

# Check Riku logs
ssh deploy@your-server-ip "tail ~/.riku/logs/myapp/*.log"

docs.title: What's Next?
docs.text:
• Installation Guide - Detailed installation instructions
  • Configuration - Configure your apps
  • Environment Variables - All available env vars
  • Scaling - Scale your applications

Congratulations! You've deployed your first app with Riku! 🎉
